stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Germany calls reports Russian soldiers are being trained in China ‘deeply disturbing’
stories.summary: German foreign ministry summons China’s ambassador and says anything that enables Russia to continue its war of aggression represents a ‘threat’ to Germany’s security. What we know on day 1,592 The Chinese ambassador in Berlin has been summoned for urgent talks about media reports that Russian soldiers were being trained in China , the German foreign ministry said on Friday. These “deeply disturbing” reports point to support for Russia from Chinese state actors, in particular the Chinese People’

stories.title: America’s accountability crisis is coming for Europe too
stories.summary: Ivo Daalder, a former U.S. ambassador to NATO, is a senior fellow at Harvard University’s Belfer Center and host of the weekly podcast “World Review with Ivo Daalder.” He writes POLITICO’s From Across the Pond column . We are living through the worst crisis in transatlantic relations since the end of World War II. We have the data to prove it. The annual Atlas of Impunity , of which I serve on the advisory board, ranks 172 countries by a single measure: how far the power of the state operates wi

stories.title: Will America Survive Another 250 Years? We Asked 11 Historians.
stories.summary: From a fledgling republic to a global superpower, the United States has constantly reinvented itself over the last 250 years. Americans have expanded democracy and driven innovation. They’ve also confronted war, economic upheaval and long struggles over equality and justice. Now, the country faces a new era of uncertainty: Americans are debating the United States’ role in the world, losing faith in its institutions and grappling with deep political and cultural divisions. In this moment, the sem

stories.title: Malta’s ‘trial of the century’ revives interest in murder of Daphne Caruana Galizia
stories.summary: Investigative journalist had enemies in high places and accused, Yorgen Fenech, is establishment figure from powerful family On the steps of the courts of justice in Valletta, there is a bronze statue of Malta’s late president. A bundle of papers in one hand, Guido de Marco stands on a plinth, looking out at passersby in the busy street below. Every morning since Wednesday this week, his daughter has walked up the steps of the same courthouse, in dark glasses and smart clothes, a bundle of paper

stories.title: Kielce pogrom in Poland: 80th anniversary of 1946 massacre
stories.summary: Just over a year after World War II ended, a mob in the Polish city of Kielce killed around 40 Jews, some of them Holocaust survivors. A lie about ritual child murder escalated the violence.

stories.title: Two Romanians jailed over stabbing of Iranian TV journalist in London
stories.summary: Judge says evidence indicates attack on Pouria Zeraati outside home was carried out on behalf of Tehran regime Two Romanians who took part in a “targeted” knife attack on a television journalist in London “on behalf of the Iranian state” have been jailed. Pouria Zeraati, who worked for the Persian-language channel Iran International, which is critical of the Tehran regime, was left bleeding in the street after being stabbed three times outside his home in Wimbledon. Continue reading...

stories.title: Polish PM warns critical months ahead in face of Russian threat
stories.summary: Tusk says Poland is preparing for "various" scenarios after media reports of a planned Russian attack.

stories.summary: Az Európai Élelmiszerbiztonsági Hatóság szerint Magyarország is érintett.

stories.title: A fizetésképtelenség határára sodródott Zebegény
stories.summary: A polgármester és a testület vitája után a Magyar Államkincstár felfüggesztette a finanszírozást, veszélyben az önkormányzati dolgozók fizetése.

stories.title: Háromszor futottam neki hóban-jégben az egyik legszebb norvég sziget sarki fényeinek
stories.summary: Nem volt egyszerű feljutni a meredek, jegesre fújt hófalon, de fent is olyan erős széllökések voltak, hogy többször kellett térdre vagy négykézláb vetődni, hogy ne fújjon le a szél a gerincről.

stories.title: Évekig élt Amerikában, most Kolozsváron van otthon, és félmilliós követőjű YouTube-csatornáján angolul magyarázza a Balkánt
stories.summary: A vajdasági származású Dunai János szerint a transzszilván valóság sokkal élhetőbb, mint az USA vagy a turisták által elfoglalt Budapest. A Living Ironically in Europe csatorna alapítója Erdélyből magyarázza el a világnak, hogy a kelet-európaiak egyszerre zseniálisak és teljesen diszfunkcionálisak.

stories.title: Egy pletyka miatt 40 holokauszttúlélő zsidót vertek halálra egy lengyel kisvárosban a világháború után
stories.summary: 80 évvel ezelőtt egy elcsavargott nyolcéves lengyel kisfiúról három napon át nem tudtak a szülei. Hazatérve, az apai pofonoktól félve azt találta ki, hogy zsidók fogságában volt, de megszökött. Szörnyű tragédiát indított el a vallomása.

stories.title: Majdnem 40 év, gátak és hűtőtornyok kellettek az új szlovák atomreaktor beindításához
stories.summary: A Garam vizére támaszkodva válhat áramexportőrré Szlovákia az új atomreaktor üzembehelyezésével. A mohi négyes blokk majdnem világrekorder lett az építési idő hossza alapján, és a költség is elszállt.

stories.title: How many more Lyhannas must there be before France takes child sexual assault seriously? | Rokhaya Diallo
stories.summary: The outrage following the 11-year-old girl’s killing is justified. But the case reveals a deeper systemic neglect of child protection When the body of an 11-year-old girl was found in a disused grain silo on a farm in the Gers region of south-west France last month, the news sent shockwaves across the country . Lyhanna had been missing for nearly a week . Members of the public had been out combing the area. Suspicion quickly focused on Jérôme Barella, the 41-year-old father of one of Lyhanna’s c

stories.title: A Magyar Turisztikai Ügynökség igazgatóságából is visszahívták Guller Zoltánt
stories.summary: Három napja már a Szerencsejáték Zrt. éléről is leváltották az Orbán családdal jó viszonyt ápoló Gullert.

stories.title: Magyar Péter hiába reménykedett, nincsenek már meg a régi megyetáblák
stories.summary: A Magyar Közút az RTL-nek mondta el, mi lett a 2023-ban a vármegyésítés miatt leszerelt táblákkal.

stories.title: German riot police clash with protesters hoping to block far-right AfD conference
stories.summary: Thousands of police deployed to Erfurt in central Germany as AfD holds conference on key Nazi date Riot police have clashed with opponents of the far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) party on the streets of Erfurt in Germany, where thousands met to block roads and prevent AfD delegates from attending the party’s biennial national conference to elect its leadership. Police reported 20,000 protesters were demonstrating in the eastern city, where Alice Weidel and Tino Chrupalla are expected

stories.title: How Trump took over America’s 250th
stories.summary: America turns 250 on Saturday. But the celebration Americans will see isn’t quite the one organizers spent the last decade planning. For nearly 10 years, the bipartisan, congressionally chartered America250 envisioned a once-in-a-generation civic commemoration built around history, service and local communities. One America250 planning document imagined transforming the tiny Badlands town of Medora, North Dakota — population roughly 150 — into a living museum recreating Theodore Roosevelt’s

stories.title: Ukrainian woman wanted after Monaco attack
stories.summary: A 39-year-old Ukrainian woman is wanted for having allegedly planted a parcel bomb in central Monaco earlier this week, gravely wounding three people. The suspect has been identified as Anastasiia Berezovska, according to an Interpol notice issued on Friday. Monaco’s head prosecutor Stéphane Thibault said on Friday that one of the three victims remained in life-threatening condition. Thibault said the suspect, whose last known address was in Germany, was specifically targeting the three ma

stories.title: Celtic nations begin to plan for breakup of UK in event of Reform election win
stories.summary: Politicians brace for constitutional turmoil if Nigel Farage’s party end up in government – or even as a strong opposition The rise of Nigel Farage has prompted political leaders across Ireland, Northern Ireland, Scotland and Wales to game the unthinkable: the breakup of the United Kingdom. Unionists who wish to save the union and nationalists who wish to end it are bracing for constitutional turmoil if Reform UK emerges triumphant – with Farage as prime minister or official leader of the opposi

stories.title: Trump plans record fireworks show; internal documents warn of smoky skies
stories.summary: The U.S. National Park Service on Saturday will launch over 850,000 fireworks into the skies of Washington, bathing the capital in patriotic blasts of light for the nation’s 250th birthday. Then comes the fallout. Scientists have well documented the soot and heavy metal pollution that can spike severely in the wake of pyrotechnic displays, and the  world-record-setting  number of fireworks lined up for July 4 will mean an equally large jump in pollution, according to experts and

stories.title: Thousands of protesters in Germany rally against far-right AfD
stories.summary: The Alternative for Germany (AfD) party’s annual congress started Saturday in the city of Erfurt with thousands of protesters gathered against the rising German far-right movement. The demonstrators sought to blockade roads used to access the conference, local police said, though this did not prevent the event from starting on time. Video footage posted to social media showed police clashing with a group of protesters flying antifa flags. “To the troublemakers out there at the door:

stories.title: EU sanctions individuals behind Alexei Navalny poisoning
stories.summary: The Council of the European Union on Friday announced sanctions targeting six scientists and researchers suspected of being involved in the poisoning of Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny. “The EU remains fully committed to countering the proliferation and use of chemical weapons,” read the Council statement . The sanctions include an asset freeze and a travel ban to the EU. Navalny, long seen as the most significant political opponent to President Vladimir Putin, died in a Rus

stories.title: Chris Minns says Labor needs to ‘climb Everest’ to stay in power as One Nation looms over state conference
stories.summary: The New South Wales premier warns of rising threat of Pauline Hanson’s party as he addresses party conference Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The New South Wales premier, Chris Minns, has admitted the Labor party needs to “climb Everest” to stay in power amid the rising threat posed by One Nation ahead of next year’s state election. But while Minns received a standing ovation as he circled Sydney’s town hall before addressing the NSW Labor conference on Saturday, he

stories.title: Overseas education project for women and girls axed by UK after two years
stories.summary: The programme, aimed at keeping 1m girls in school across Africa, Asia and the Middle East, withdrawn after aid cuts A leading higher education programme, aimed at keeping 1 million girls in school across Africa, Asia and the Middle East, has been axed by the British government just two years after it was announced. The scheme, Strengthening higher education for female empowerment (SHEFE), which was unveiled with some fanfare two years ago by the outgoing Conservative government, had a £45m budg

stories.title: Trump launches America’s 250th birthday celebrations with partisan attack
stories.summary: In a speech at Mount Rushmore, the US president claimed a resurgent ‘communist menace’ posed a severe threat to the country Donald Trump has kicked off America’s 250th birthday weekend with an extraordinary partisan attack on the “communist menace” in America, framing its supporters as “the enemy of July 4th 1776”. The US president spoke for half an hour on Friday night at Mount Rushmore in South Dakota, the latest stop on his tour celebrating the milestone anniversary of the US Declaration of I

stories.title: UK woman, 21, arrested in Thailand after allegedly stabbing boyfriend to death
stories.summary: Officers found the body of a 34-year-old man in a luxury rental home in the Pattaya area, local media says A 21-year-old British woman has been arrested in Thailand after allegedly fatally stabbing her boyfriend, according to local media reports. The Bangkok Post reported that on Thursday morning local time, officers found the body of a 34-year-old man, who operated a cannabis farm, in a luxury rental home in the Pattaya area, a beachside region two hours outside of Bangkok known for its large e

stories.title: OpenAI’s apparent failure to visit key site raises questions over UK investment
stories.summary: Exclusive: £20bn of ‘potential’ £30bn AI investment touted by UK ministers appears to have been hypothetical It was to be the biggest undertaking in Britain for OpenAI, the company behind ChatGPT. Stargate UK – a multibillion-pound UK datacentre project – would represent “a major step forward in the US-UK technology partnership”. But the plans were paused in April , with an OpenAI spokesperson citing concerns over regulation and high energy costs . Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘The situation is terrible’: aid workers on life in Sudanese city pummelled by drone strikes
stories.summary: El Obeid becomes key battleground in war between Sudan’s armed forces and their paramilitary enemies, the RSF Fatima is losing count of the number of drone attacks on the besieged city of El Obeid in Sudan, but says the attacks this past weekend were the most violent so far. The drones hit schools and fuel stations, killing more than 20 people, including students, she says. “Over the past few months, seeing 40 or 45 drones is the norm. You can literally count them,” said the aid volunteer, whose

stories.title: Ukraine strikes oil terminals near St. Petersburg in Russia
stories.summary: The long-range strikes are the latest salvo in Ukraine’s expanding campaign to inflict economic damage on Russia and hinder its war machine.

stories.title: Welcoming the Taliban? — Germany's new Afghanistan policy
stories.summary: To facilitate the deportation of criminals to Afghanistan, the German government is allowing Taliban diplomats into the country.

stories.title: Nigeria: US pulls some troops after anti-IS operation
stories.summary: The United States has withdrawn most of the forces it deployed for a recent operation against "Islamic State" militants in northeastern Nigeria, but it is continuing to provide intelligence support at Abuja's request.

stories.title: Ukraine hits major oil terminal in Russia's St Petersburg
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says the target is key infrastructure "that generates revenue for Russia's war".

stories.title: Third teenager charged after 15-year-old Melbourne boy allegedly left to die from stab wounds
stories.summary: Darweish Mohamed was found critically wounded outside Craigieburn community hospital on Wednesday evening Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A third teenager has been charged over the fatal stabbing of a 15-year-old boy found outside a Melbourne hospital. A 14-year-old from Victoria’s Hume area was arrested on Friday evening and was charged with murder, police said.He has been scheduled to appear before a children’s court. Continue reading...

stories.title: Sydney records hottest June since 1859 as expert warns new high a ‘signature’ of global warming
stories.summary: Bureau of Meteorology says city’s mean temperature reached 16.1C, surpassing the previous record of 15.7C set in 1991 Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Sydney has officially experienced its hottest June since records began, after a winter month of spring-like warmth. While early weather data suggested the month was merely among the warmest, the Bureau of Meteorology confirmed on Friday that June 2026 broke the all-time mean temperature record at Observatory Hill, where

stories.title: As the US marks 250, does the special relationship still exist – or is the UK just irrelevant?
stories.summary: The gap between America and Britain has grown economically as Trump asserts ‘the UK is dying’. Culturally, however, it’s a different story On 1 June 1785 John Adams travelled to London to become the first US ambassador to Britain, in which capacity he was to meet George III. By his own admission, Adams trembled at the encounter. After all, it had been less than a decade since he helped Thomas Jefferson write the Declaration of Independence denouncing the king as an absolute “tyrant” who had “plu

stories.title: ‘Trump accounts’: Wall Street-backed investment funds for children to go live
stories.summary: Savings plans for children born between January 2025 and December 2028 launched as president seeks electoral boost Trump accounts, a savings vehicle named after the US president and authorized by congressional Republicans, are set to go live on Saturday, offering American parents a new way to save money for their children by investing in funds managed by major Wall Street firms. All accounts established for children born between January 2025 through December 2028 – nearly the entirety of Donald

stories.title: ‘Emotional connection’: Wonderwall becomes England’s World Cup anthem
stories.summary: Oasis tune has been sung from Texas to Massachusetts and soon in Mexico City – and the players have joined in too It has become England’s World Cup anthem more three decades after it was first released, being belted out by fans from Texas to Massachusetts. Wonderwall by Oasis will soon be heard in Mexico City too, where the Three Lions will face the tournament co-hosts Mexico on Sunday evening – or at 1am on Monday for fans singing along back home. Continue reading...

stories.title: Birdsong data from Merlin ID app to help global biodiversity project
stories.summary: Cornell Lab for Ornithology plans data linkup between app and population monitoring on eBird platform The Merlin bird ID app will allow users to feed real-time bird identifications into one of the world’s biggest citizen-science biodiversity projects in an update it is hoped will aid conservation of at-risk birds. Since 2021, the free Merlin app, created by the Cornell Lab for Ornithology, has used machine learning to provide an almost instantaneous sound-identification service for birdsong, alo

stories.title: Pope praises US history of welcoming migrants in implicit rebuke to Trump
stories.summary: Pope Leo urges Americans to live up to ideals of Declaration of Independence in first key address to US Pope Leo has used his first key address to his home country to praise the US history of ⁠welcoming migrants, urging Americans to live up to the ideals put forward in the ⁠Declaration of Independence. In his latest implicit rebuke to Donald Trump, the first US leader of the Roman Catholic church said the word “America” had become ‌a “byword for freedom” across the world because of the way the c

stories.title: How the 1986 Mexico World Cup was almost cancelled after a devastating earthquake
stories.summary: Guardian reports after the disaster told of 5,000 deaths, much of the capital being razed, and doubts about Mexico hosting the finals Mexico last hosted the World Cup in 1986 , but the competition was almost cancelled several months before the start when an earthquake struck the capital, Mexico City, leaving at least 5,000 people dead, 30,000 homeless and much of the city flattened, in one of the worst earthquakes to hit the country. To this day, the death toll remains disputed, with some estima

stories.title: Third of disadvantaged white pupils in England leave primary school without being able to read properly
stories.summary: Exclusive: Analysis finds lower reading fluency than children from other ethnic backgrounds and richer peers A third of disadvantaged white pupils in England leave their primary school unable to read well enough to access the secondary curriculum, leading to disengagement and school absence, according to new research. The findings were published days after an independent inquiry into white working-class educational outcomes concluded the current education system was “not set up to serve white wo

stories.title: Mayfair casino facing legal action after former waiter says he did not get fair share of tips
stories.summary: Former worker at Metropolitan on Park Lane in London says he had to share tips with managers and other staff A Mayfair casino is facing legal action from a former waiter who says he was not given a fair share of tips. The former worker at the Metropolitan Mayfair – part of the Metropolitan Casinos group owned by the US investment firm Silver Point Capital, which operates seven casinos in the UK and four in Egypt – has filed a complaint with the employment tribunal in London over his share of cas

stories.title: Three-year-old injured in UK crocodile enclosure faces ‘long rehabilitation’
stories.summary: Boy who was allegedly thrown into the area at a Cambridgeshire zoo has undergone five surgeries A three-year-old boy left seriously injured after being allegedly thrown into a crocodile enclosure has undergone five surgeries and faces a long “rehabilitation journey”. His family, who provided the update, also thanked donors who had raised more than £25,000. His parents said they had been “living at the hospital” since the incident at Johnsons of Old Hurst farm and zoo, in Cambridgeshire, on 18 Ju

stories.title: Crowds gather as six-day funeral for former Iranian supreme leader begins
stories.summary: Up to 30 million people expected to attend delayed events for Ali Khamenei, killed at start of war with US and Israel Huge crowds gathered at the funeral of the former Iranian supreme leader once the gates of the sprawling Grand Mosalla mosque in central Tehran let in thousands of mourners who had been waiting through the night to enter the grounds. Iran is staging mass funeral processions for Ali Khamenei – whose ⁠37-year reign was brought ⁠to an ​end in February by the first airstrike of the w

stories.title: Ali Khamenei’s six-day funeral expected to draw millions in Iran
stories.summary: Country’s leadership vows to never surrender as memorial on grand scale aims to relay message of resistance to world In the small hours of Friday the police roadblocks, stalls, posters and army vans were starting to appear across Tehran, as millions of Iranians prepared to attend the long-delayed six-day funeral ceremony for Ali Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader for 36 turbulent years. Khamenei was killed aged 86 in the opening salvo of the US-Israeli attack on the country in February, and the fin

stories.title: One Nation is campaigning directly to Christians. But will party policies rub against worshippers’ conscience?
stories.summary: Anti-abortion policies may have appeal, but with one in three Australian churchgoers born overseas, talk of a monoculture may put them off Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast When One Nation recruit Barnaby Joyce addressed anti-abortion campaigners at a Sydney rally in early June, the former deputy prime minister told the audience he could see “about 1,500 people who can hand out how to vote cards”. Christian leaders spoke at the rally. The Lord’s Prayer was recited. Man

stories.title: New pipeline in Canada to proceed after C$150bn pledged to ease BC and First Nations concerns
stories.summary: Port expansion and protections for whales part of BC and Alberta plan to expand country’s presence overseas The governments of Canada and the province of Alberta will move forward on a major new oil pipeline after the pair announced a plan to ease concerns of British Columbia and First Nations on the Pacific coast. Canada’s prime minister, Mark Carney, shuttled between British Columbia and Alberta on Thursday to announce more than C$150bn in new investments in both provinces, part of a broader p

stories.title: Heat wave upends July Fourth celebrations across US
stories.summary: A dangerous heat wave has disrupted July Fourth events across the United States, including Donald Trump's flagship Great American State Fair, as the nation prepares to mark its 250th birthday.

stories.title: Iran: Khamenei's funeral ceremonies officially begin
stories.summary: Huge crowds have gathered in Tehran to pay their final respects to Iran's supreme leader, who was killed in an airstrike at the start of the US-Israeli campaign against Iran.

stories.title: US: President Trump pardons 11 people, including Clean Air Act violators
stories.summary: Donald Trump has granted pardons to nine people convicted of violating a key environmental law. The US President has also pardoned an associate of convicted felon and former lobbyist Jack Abramoff.

stories.title: Trump set to put NATO unity to the test at Turkey summit
stories.summary: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte is working to keep the alliance united and Donald Trump engaged. But disputes over burden sharing, US military commitments, and the Iran war are creating a major test for NATO unity.

stories.title: Australia's Great Barrier Reef again avoids 'in danger' label
stories.summary: UNESCO acknowledged Australia's efforts towards the Great Barrier Reef's health but raised concerns over mass bleaching. For now, the popular marine site seems to have dodged the "endangered" tag.

stories.title: Germany news: AfD kicks off conference amid massive protests
stories.summary: As members of the far-right AfD party met in Erfurt, thousands of protesters were marching and blocking roads across the city. Meanwhile, firefighters were struggling to contain a major blaze in Stuttgart. DW has more.

stories.title: India news: Government lists 23 Pakistanis as 'terrorists'
stories.summary: Indian government has designated 23 Pakistan-based individuals as "terrorists" under its anti-terror law. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Pope Leo calls on Europe to do more for migrants
stories.summary: The pontiff's trip to the migration frontline of Lampedusa underscored a stark message for Europe and the United States as intolerance and indifference rise.

stories.title: Germany: Merz rebukes Trump jab over defense at Baltic event
stories.summary: In a meeting with Baltic leaders, Germany's Chancellor Friedrich Merz played up Germany's defense spending. It comes after new criticism from President Donald Trump that Germany is lagging behind other NATO allies.

stories.title: Pope Leo defends US immigrant history at 250th anniversary
stories.summary: The first US pope said "successive waves of immigrants" played a role in shaping the United States, which marks its 250th anniversary on Saturday. Leo's remarks have made him a target of President Donald Trump.

stories.title: Taylor Swift marries Travis Kelce in NYC ceremony officiated by Adam Sandler
stories.summary: The star-studded celebration saw several streets in Midtown Manhattan shut, as hordes of fans gathered outside the arena.

stories.title: Brutal heat cancels Fourth of July events, from DC to Philadelphia
stories.summary: More than 165 million people are enduring dangerous heat and humidity, with temperatures expected to stay at 38C (100F) for several days.

stories.title: Watch: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wed in NYC - here's how the day unfolded
stories.summary: The couple tied the knot in a star-studded ceremony officiated by comedian Adam Sandler at Madison Square Garden.

stories.title: 'Vanishingly rare' copy of US Declaration of Independence discovered in the UK
stories.summary: Graham Moore, the Revolution 250 curator at the National Archives, said the "exceptional" item was "one of the rarest forms of the Declaration we know about".

stories.title: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
stories.summary: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.

stories.title: Cape Verde fans stand proud despite Argentina defeat
stories.summary: Cape Verde supporters expressed pride in their team's historic World Cup campaign despite a 3-2 extra-time defeat to defending champions Argentina.

stories.title: Iranians mourn as Khamenei funeral ceremonies begin
stories.summary: Large crowds gathered in Tehran as Iran began six days of funeral ceremonies for late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, who was killed during the US-Israeli conflict.

stories.title: Large crowds gather in Tehran on first day of Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral
stories.summary: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla until Monday, before travelling across Iran and Iraq.

stories.title: Trump warns of communist threat as Washington parade cancelled
stories.summary: "Communism is a mortal threat to American liberty," Trump said during a speech. "It is the greatest threat to our country".

stories.title: Pope urges Europe to do more for migrants while visiting gateway island
stories.summary: The pontiff pays tribute to migrants who lost their lives at sea at a cemetery on the Italian island of Lampedusa.

stories.title: Dior dress, Adam Sandler and a man of honour: What we know about Taylor and Travis's wedding
stories.summary: The couple tied the knot in New York on Friday, after months of speculation about when, where and how it would happen.

stories.title: German row over plan for workers to need sick note on first day of illness
stories.summary: A doctors' group says it "borders on madness" that patients will have to obtain the note in person.

stories.title: Iran FM confirms reports Israel planned to target Tehran's negotiators
stories.summary: Iran's foreign minister corroborated US media reports that Washington warned Tehran Israel may have had its chief negotiators on a target list during April talks in Islamabad, the same talks that produced the framework deal signed on 17 June.

stories.title: Zuma showing South Africa 'middle finger' by meeting Gupta brother - minister
stories.summary: Former President Zuma's trip to India reignites anger over a decade-old corruption scandal.

stories.title: Did he say 'Bismillah'? Cristiano Ronaldo’s penalty whisper ignites global debate
stories.summary: Superstar striker Cristiano Ronaldo’s whisper broadcast live around the world before the decisive penalty against Croatia sparked a global debate over whether it was an Arabic-inspired sacred invocation or his own pre-Saudi encouragement routine in Portuguese.

stories.title: Conservative candidate Keiko Fujimori wins Peru's presidential election in runoff
stories.summary: Voters were primarily concerned with increasing levels of crime, especially extortion by violent organized crime gangs, and Fujimori pledged to combat crime with an iron fist.

stories.title: 'Flamingo Revolution' takes off as thousands demand Albanian PM's resignation
stories.summary: Flamingos have become a national symbol of protest, triggered by opposition to a luxury development.

stories.title: Turkish comedian arrested for 'insulting' Islam and Erdogan
stories.summary: Turkish comedian Deniz Göktaş was arrested at Istanbul Airport on Thursday following a stand-up show posted to YouTube. The 32-year-old faces multiple probes, including "publicly insulting religious values" and insulting President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan.

stories.title: Spain’s conservatives and far-right Vox increase ties with Andalucía coalition
stories.summary: Deal including ‘national priority’ policy brings prospect closer of countrywide agreement between parties The prospect of a national coalition between Spain’s conservative People’s party (PP) and the far-right Vox party has drawn closer still after the two groupings sealed another deal that will allow the PP to continue ruling the southern region of Andalucía. The PP, which has governed the former socialist bastion for the past seven years, lost its absolute majority in May’s regional election ,

stories.title: Cold feet and cooling prices: Australia’s property market is transforming – and first home buyers aren’t biting
stories.summary: Exclusive: Data shows buyers are not rushing to get into the market, despite lower prices and less competition Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast First home buyers are starting to step back and investor demand is slumping in all areas except new property, as Australia’s housing market enters a downturn. Research shows the market is transforming, nearly two months on from a third consecutive interest rate rise and sweeping tax reforms. Continue reading...

stories.title: Deaths in France surged 30% during hottest week of record June heatwave
stories.summary: Public health authority says 2,025 excess deaths probably an underestimate and that it expects toll to rise further The number of deaths recorded in France surged by nearly 30% during the hottest week of the record-breaking heatwave that scorched much of Europe last month, the public health authority has said, adding that it expected the toll to rise further. Public Health France said on Friday there had been “an increase of 29.1%, corresponding to 2,025 additional deaths compared with the previ

stories.title: Pope Leo praises US history of welcoming immigrants at 250th anniversary – video
stories.summary: Pope Leo has used his first major address to his home country to praise the US's history of ⁠welcoming immigrants, urging Americans to live up to the ideals put forward in the ⁠declaration of independence. In a speech broadcast live from the Vatican to the National Constitution Center in Philadelphia ‌as he received the Center’s liberty medal, Leo said he hoped that ideals ​of 'unity, justice and peace' held by the founding fathers would guide the US as it celebrated its 250th anniversary Pope p

stories.title: Free electricity in Australia, and the week's other eco wins
stories.summary: Germans save an ancient woodland from coal mining, Australia gives away surplus solar power, and how one city became the world's best place for cyclists?

stories.title: EU sanctions Russian researchers involved in Navalny's death
stories.summary: The six sanctioned Russian scientists were linked to the production of epibatidine — the deadly toxin identified in the body of Putin critic Alexei Navalny after his death.

stories.title: NATO summit: European members and Canada pledge long-term Ukraine aid
stories.summary: The NATO alliance's European members and Canada have signaled a readiness to fully take over the financing of Ukraine's fight against Russia.

stories.title: Maltese tycoon faces trial over Caruana Galizia murder
stories.summary: The Maltese businessman accused of masterminding journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia’s 2017 murder is in court — seven years after his arrest. Five other men have already been found guilty for their roles in the murder.

stories.title: Lyra McKee: 3 men acquitted of killing N. Irish journalist
stories.summary: McKee was killed while covering clashes between Republicans and police in 2019. Judge Patricia Smyth said evidence was insufficient for a conviction, while McKee's family expressed disappointment.

stories.title: NASA launches spacecraft to recover sinking telescope
stories.summary: The Swift telescope has been affected by recent solar storms and is sinking faster than ever, heading toward Earth. A NASA mission will try to capture it and boost its orbit.

stories.title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce tie knot in star-studded ceremony
stories.summary: The couple got married in a major star-studded celebration at Madison Square Garden over the Fourth of July weekend.

stories.title: Video shows moment 11-year-old drives pickup truck into Buddhist procession
stories.summary: Ten people were killed after an 11-year-old boy drove a pickup truck into a Buddhist procession in northeastern Thailand.

stories.title: Overcrowded bus plunges into ravine in southwest Pakistan, killing 40 passengers
stories.summary: Pakistan’s President Asif Ali Zardari expressed sorrow over the crash, offered condolences to the families of those killed and wished the injured a speedy recovery.

stories.title: Instagram running ads promoting child sexual abuse material in India, BBC finds
stories.summary: The ads use terms including “rape” and “child video” and link to content on the messaging app Telegram.

stories.title: Ukrainian suspect hunted by police after Monaco bomb attack was 'disguised as a man'
stories.summary: Officials believe the suspect may not have acted alone and spent days casing out the scene of the attack.

stories.title: Mourners in Damascus bury cafe bomb blast victims as officials promise justice
stories.summary: Although there is so far no indication who carried out the attack, many were quick to blame loyalists of former President Bashar al-Assad, who was ousted in an insurgent offensive in December 2024.

stories.title: Dissident Hong Kong bookseller Lam Wing-kee dies aged 70
stories.summary: Lam Wing-kee, who defied China by selling material critical of Beijing, passed away after suffering from lung cancer.

stories.title: 'It's so hot' - Dangerous hot weather grips parts of the US
stories.summary: More than 165 million people were sweltering on Friday under record temperatures along the US East Coast and Midwest, according to the US National Weather Service.

stories.title: Anguished families left to identify Venezuela quake victims at makeshift morgue
stories.summary: The disaster has overwhelmed local services, with bodies put outside or in tents for identification.

stories.title: More cows than pupils - what is behind mass school closures in rural Kenya?
stories.summary: More than 2,000 primary schools are facing closure as enrolment numbers plummet.

stories.title: Row after Indian state drops eggs from school lunch menu
stories.summary: West Bengal's move to replace eggs in some school meals has sparked a wider debate over nutrition and choice.

stories.title: Kolumbia–Ghána 1-0
Hogyan lehet a felső középosztály gyerekeit kevésbé szelektív iskolarendszerben tartani?
stories.summary: A hat- és nyolcosztályos gimnáziumok erősítik a társadalmi szelekciót, de pedagógiailag előnyösebbek lehetnek a 8+4-es modellnél – mondja Horn Dániel oktatáskutató közgazdász.

stories.title: A tengerfenéktől az űrig erősítené az EU védelmi iparát az Európai Bizottság
stories.summary: Az EU vezető testülete meghatározta a Közös Érdekű Európai Védelmi Projektek öt pillérét.

stories.title: Egy kis robot fogja megmenteni a NASA Föld felé araszoló űrteleszkópját
stories.summary: A Swift egyre közeledik a Föld felszínéhez, pedig még nem jött el az ideje, hogy felmondja a szolgálatot. A NASA egy hűtőszekrény méretű robotot küld utána, hogy felemelje 600 kilométeres magasságig.

stories.title: Kolumbia legyőzte Ghánát, teljes a vb-nyolcaddöntők mezőnye
stories.summary: Arias gólja döntött, az afrikai csapat nem találta el a kaput.

stories.title: Az Anthropic beszáll a gyógyszeriparba is
stories.summary: Az egyik legnagyobb MI-cég bejelentette, hogy gyógyszereket fog tervezni olyan betegségekre, amikre kevés kutatási figyelem jut.

stories.title: Itt a nyolcaddöntők menetrendje, klasszikusok jönnek
stories.summary: Szombat este a franciák már a pályán lesznek, a brazilok vasárnap este játszanak, az angolok hétfőre virradóra. Portugál-spanyol lesz hétfő este. Kedden három meccsel alakul ki a legjobb nyolc mezőnye.

stories.title: Messi: Nem becsültük le őket
stories.summary: Az argentin csapatkapitány kritikusan nyilatkozott a zöld-fokiak ellen a hosszabbításban elért 3-2-es győzelem után.

stories.title: Nyomoz a rendőrség a Tiborcz-féle buszos cég egyik közbeszerzése ügyében
stories.summary: A Pannon Busz-Rent idén tavasszal nyert meg egy közbeszerzést, a közlekedési minisztérium új vezetése szerint felmerültek jogsértésre utaló jelek.

stories.title: Medveintervenciós csapat védi Tusnádfürdőt a legádázabb medvéktől
stories.summary: Hogyan védekeznek a helyiek a ragadozók ellen? A Deutsche Welle riportjából kiderül.

stories.title: A velünk élő azbeszt
stories.summary: Nem rettegni kell a természettől, hanem tanulni a mostani azbesztproblémából is, mondják a geológusok, és minden tudományos kérdésre választ adnak, ami az azbesztbotrányban felmerül.

stories.title: Visky András regénye, a Kitelepítés nyerte a Nemzetközi Irodalmi Díjat
stories.summary: Az író és a gulágregényt németre fordító Tankó Tímea péntek este vette át az elismerést Berlinben.

stories.title: Adam Sandler adta össze Taylor Swiftet és Travis Kelce-t
stories.summary: A pár sajtóközleményt adott ki az esküvőről nem sokkal a boldogító igen után.

stories.title: Az oroszok azt állítják, elfoglaltak egy fontos donbászi várost, az elemzők szerint ez valószínűtlen
stories.summary: Putyinra hivatkozva a Kreml szóvivője azt mondta, Kosztyantinyivka városa most már orosz kézen van, de ezt az ukránok nem erősítették meg. Szakértők is kétkedve fogadták a hírt.

stories.title: Megható üzenettel köszönt el Messi a zöld-foki csodakapus Vozinhától
stories.summary: A lefújás után váltottak pár szót egymással.

stories.title: Nyílt pályázaton választanának új kuratóriumi tagokat az alapítványi egyetemekhez
stories.summary: Az alapítványi fenntartási rendszer egyéves átmeneti időszakban még változatlanul fennmarad, hogy biztosítsák az intézmények stabil működését.

stories.title: Harmincnégy éve még az Edda és egy simlis cég közösen akarta megmenteni a Velencei-tavat
stories.summary: A kilencvenes évek elején az Edda és a zenekar szponzora még egy alapítványt is létrehozott a cél érdekében. Később jól összevesztek, de a régi sztori szinte mindegyik mellékszála érdekes.

stories.title: Nem lehet fürödni a Szelidi-tóban
stories.summary: A magas vízhőmérséklet miatt elszaporodott egy baktérium, ezért a strand zárva tart.

stories.title: Hankó Balázs alig pár nappal a választások előtt még kiszórt 400 milliót miniszteri hatáskörben
stories.summary: A 444 kikérte a támogatottak pontos listáját. Milliók jutottak volna a Pesti Srácok videóit készítő cégnek és fideszes pártemberek alapítványainak.

stories.title: Ötórás káosz után döntött a FIFA, nem tolja el az Anglia–Mexikó időpontját
stories.summary: A vihar veszélye fennáll, de a csapatok sem értettek egyet azzal, hogy hat órával korábban, vagyis délben kezdjenek.

stories.title: Lebontják a Kelenföldi Erőmű 146 méter magas kéményét
stories.summary: Ezzel az utolsó része is eltűnik az erőmű 70-es évekbeli ipari technológiájának, amely nem kapott műemléki besorolást.

stories.title: Öt nap alatt 10 ezer embert tartóztatott le az ICE
stories.summary: Az amerikai bevándorlási hivatal a látványosabb akciók helyett most a fű alatti intézkedésekre koncentrál, de jelentősen megnövelte a letartóztatások számát.

stories.title: Nagyon könnyű volt úgy természetvédelemmel foglalkozni, hogy folyamatosan ellenzékben voltunk
stories.summary: Nagyon felszabadító érzés, ami a természetvédelem területén történt a választás óta eltelt két és fél hónapban, mondták a civil szervezetek képviselői a Restart fesztiválon Kisapátiban. Ennek ellenére a kormányváltás után is fontosnak tartják megőrizni azt a kritikus és független attitűdöt, amit az elmúlt 16 évben tanúsítottak.

stories.title: Megsorozta Ukrajna drónokkal Oroszországot, Szentpétervárt is találat érte
stories.summary: Egy kikötőt, olajfinomítót és gyárat is találtak el ukrán drónok, Zelenszkij szerint olyan területeket támadtak, amik a háborút szolgálják ki.

stories.title: Vöröspandakölyök született a Fővárosi Állatkertben
stories.summary: A vöröspandák életük első hónapjaiban szinte végig az odúban maradnak, ezért a gondozóknak csak nehezen sikerült lefotózniuk a kölyköt.

stories.title: Jó esély van argentin–francia döntőre, de azért még sokan beleszólhatnak
stories.summary: Már csak tizenhat csapat van versenyben a foci-világbajnokságon, az egyenes kieséses szakasz első köre után Európa és Afrika szenvedett nagy veszteségeket. A házigazdákra most vár igazán nagy kihívás, a topfavorit franciák talán szusszanhatnak, bár eddig se ez volt jellemző rájuk. Eddig is voltak meglepetések, az lenne a váratlan, ha ezek a folytatásban elmaradnának.

stories.title: XIV. Leó pápa a függetlenség napján a migráció egyik európai frontvonalára utazott, és felszólította az amerikaiakat, hogy fogadják be és védjék meg a bevándorlókat
stories.summary: Úgy döntött, hogy július 4-én a Földközi-tengeren átkelő migránsok egyik fő érkezési pontját látogatja meg, és ad egy kis iránymutatást Amerikának.

stories.title: Ancelotti talizmánja, aki még nem kapott ki világbajnokságon
stories.summary: Az olasz edzőt sokszor bírálták Casemiro miatt, most megköszönik neki, hogy Japán ellen a pályán hagyta.

stories.title: Már csak sprintfutam győzelme nem volt Antonellinek, a Brit Nagydíjon ezt is megszerezte
stories.summary: Habár a sprintidőmérőn csak a második helyet tudta megszerezni, a sprintfutamot simán nyerte.

stories.title: Több mint kétezer hektáron pusztított erdőtűz a Costa Brava közelében
stories.summary: A hatóságok szerint gondatlanság okozhatta a tüzet.

stories.title: Férfinak álcázta magát a monacói robbantás feltételezett elkövetője, az Interpol elfogatóparancsot adott ki ellene
stories.summary: Anasztaszija Berezovszka ukrán állampolgár, feltehetőleg Németországba menekült.

stories.title: Vitézy Dávid: A korrupcióval szemben zéró tolerancia van
stories.summary: A miniszter a Közlekedési és Beruházási Minisztérium állománygyűlésén azt mondta, hogy olyan minisztériumot akar létrehozni, ami minden döntését szakmai alapon, a közérdek mentén hozza meg.

stories.title: ‘I can only describe it as a war zone’: the rescuers navigating Venezuela’s post-quake hellscape
stories.summary: Thousands of volunteers are joined by overseas teams in the hope of finding more survivors in the rubble, reports Tom Phillips in Caraballeda. Photography and video by Manu Quintero When twin earthquakes tore through Venezuela’s northern coast last week, Israel Rivas was at home hundreds of miles away in the industrial city of San Félix. As the scale of the catastrophe became clear, the 24-year-old knew he had to react. A mechanic and budding photographer, Rivas gathered the money he had been sa

stories.title: Turkish comedian Deniz Goktas detained at Istanbul airport
stories.summary: Turkish stand-up comedian Deniz Goktas was detained on his return to Turkey, days after a video he posted online gained traction. The clip included references to President Recep Tayyip Erdogan and religion.

stories.title: Far-right AfD threatens eastern Germany's energy transition
stories.summary: Germany's far-right AfD wants to revive coal and nuclear power and curb non-EU immigration. Businesses in eastern Germany warn that its policies could harm the economy.

stories.title: Von der Leyen unveils major EU support initiatives in strategic trip to Armenia
stories.summary: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen announced a significant economic and political support package to Armenia during her strategic visit to engage with the South Caucasus peace process and the new opportunities in the region formerly in Russia’s orbit.

stories.title: Air conditioner rush: Scuffles break out at Lidl stores across France
stories.summary: Hundreds of shoppers queued outside Lidl supermarkets across France on Thursday after the retailer announced the sale of 200,000 fans and portable air conditioners, triggering long lines, scuffles and police intervention at several stores.

stories.title: Venezuela's interim leader defends earthquake response as death toll tops 2,500
stories.summary: Delcy Rodríguez has hit back at criticism over her government’s response to the quakes, rejecting accusations of slow rescue efforts and claims that official casualty figures have been under-reported.

stories.title: Russia's fuel crisis: Is Putin under pressure?
stories.summary: Ukraine's extended campaign against Russian energy infrastructure is disrupting fuel supplies and military logistics. With shortages mounting in occupied Crimea, is the pressure pushing Putin toward negotiations?

stories.title: At least nine monks killed in Thailand after boy drives truck into procession
stories.summary: Charges yet to be filed over incident in Mukdahan involving 11-year-old, as police seek to establish circumstances An 11-year-old boy has driven his parents’ truck into a Buddhist procession in Thailand, killing at least nine monks. CCTV footage shared by a local rescue group showed the moment the monks, wearing orange robes, were run over as they walked in procession along a road. The timestamp on the footage was shortly before 11am local time on Thursday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Canadian boy dies of rabies after waking to find bat on his face
stories.summary: Eleven-year-old developed symptoms 19 days after encounter in Ontario in ‘exceedingly rare’ case Doctors in Canada say a child who awoke to find a bat resting on his nose and mouth while visiting an Ontario cottage later died of rabies, in an “exceedingly rare case” that highlights the need for better public awareness. In a report published this week in the Canadian Medical Association Journal, infectious disease physicians confirmed that the 11-year-old boy died from rabies, a fatality they sai

stories.title: Venezuelan man saved from collapsed mall eight days after earthquakes
stories.summary: Security guard Hernán Alberto Gil Flores, 43, initially told rescuers not to tell his wife in case he did not survive A 43-year-old security guard who survived last week’s devastating earthquakes in Venezuela thanks to a pocket of air in his workstation cabin has been pulled from the collapsed basement of a shopping centre amid huge cheers from international rescue teams. Hernán Alberto Gil Flores had been trapped for eight days under the rubble of the Galerías Playa Grande in the hard-hit coast

stories.title: ‘Give him any award, and he’ll come running’: Narendra Modi racks up honours on overseas trips
stories.summary: Indian prime minister has a habit of collecting awards on his travels, some as their first and only recipient As Narendra Modi touched down in Seychelles over the weekend, the archipelago nation in the Indian Ocean swiftly bestowed one of its “highest” honours upon the Indian prime minister. Modi beamed as he accepted the Guardian of the Blue Horizon award from Patrick Herminie, the Seychelles president, complete with a trophy and certificate. Continue reading...

stories.title: Race to control wildfires in southern France – in pictures
stories.summary: Nearly 3,000 people have been evacuated in southern France as the country swelters through a record-breaking heatwave. The fire started at a campsite, destroying dozens of mobile homes before spreading to the marina area, where thick, toxic smoke blanketed boats. The fire broke out in the town of Sainte-Marie-la-Mer and spread to Canet-en-Roussillon on Thursday [• This gallery’s headline and subheading were amended on 3 July 2026; an earlier version referred to south-western France ] Continue re

stories.title: China says man who flew plane into Beijing skyscraper had mental health problems
stories.summary: Official statement offers the most detailed official account yet of the highly unusual and fatal incident in Beijing Chinese authorities said the man who flew a small plane into Beijing’s tallest skyscraper last week was a 66-year-old who had mental health problems. The statement published on Thursday offered the most detailed official account yet of the highly unusual incident that occurred in Beijing’s central business district on the evening of 26 June. Continue reading...

stories.title: Weather tracker: Heatwave breaks June temperature records across Europe
stories.summary: UK provisional peak of 37.7C shatters previous record by huge margin, while Germany hits all-time high of 41.7C Last week’s heatwave across western Europe shattered national June records and set new all-time highs. The UK recorded a provisional high of 37.7C at Lingwood in Norfolk on Friday 27 June, smashing the previous June record of 35.6C, set in 1976. Such a margin is exceptionally rare: temperature records are typically broken by 0.1C or 0.2C, not a remarkable 2.1C. Continue reading...

stories.title: Mindent kivizsgál és tudomásul veszi a gyár leállítását a debreceni Semcorp
stories.summary: Arra reagáltak, hogy a kormányhivatal tűzvédelmi hiányosságok miatt drasztikus lépést hozott.

stories.title: Kormányhivatalnokot vettek őrizetbe vesztegetés gyanújával
stories.summary: A gyanú szerint egy szabolcsi vállalkozó két elnyert pályázat után is visszacsorgatott az ügyében eljáró osztályvezetőnek.

stories.title: Foci, jég, világuralom és vérfürdő – társasjátékok a nyárra
stories.summary: Mutatunk olyan focis játékot, amit szinte egy hidratációs szünet alatt is végig lehet pörgetni, de olyan társasokat is, amikkel kitölthetők a hosszú nyári esték, akár a Földet, akár a Holdat akarjuk meghódítani.

stories.title: A jó novella ott kezdődik, ahol véget ér
stories.summary: Ez a kötet nem pusztán novellák gyűjteménye, hanem önmeghatározása szerint a „novella világtörténete”. És hogy ez mit jelent? Talán semmit, és kicsit mindent. Kritika.

stories.title: A lövészárkok ásóival nehéz lesz új korszakot nyitni – reagált Nagy Ervin Krasznahorkai visszautasítására
stories.summary: A kultúráért felelős államtitkár voltaképp egyetért a Nobel-díjas íróval, aki nem fogadta el a Nemzet Művésze címet.

stories.title: Őrsi Gergely annyira ráér a börtönben, hogy napernyőt fabrikált egy helyi szobornak
stories.summary: Nem nagynak, csak egy ilyen kicsi giccsnek, fülpiszkálóból. Azt írta, 2-3 órája ment rá.

stories.title: Az utolsó pillanatos ausztrál kapuscsere nem jött be, Egyiptom ment tovább büntetőkkel
stories.summary: Egyiptom története során először nyert vb-meccset a kieséses szakaszban, pedig még öngólt is rúgtak. 1-1 után 11-esek döntöttek.

stories.title: Argentína–Zöld-foki Köztársaság 3-2
Nagyon megizzasztották Argentínát a zöld-fokiak, de a hosszabbításban továbbment a címvédő
stories.summary: Lisandro Martínez góllal és gólpasszal jelentkezett, Lionel Messi megint gólt lőtt, de a 3-2-re nem lehetnek büszkék. A zöld-fokiak hatalmasat küzdöttek, és a végén majdnem kiharcolták a tizenegyeseket is.

stories.title: Is Mexico the 2026 World Cup's best host country?
stories.summary: Mexico has fully embraced its role as a World Cup host. Visitors are raving about the atmosphere and say they wish that there were more knockout-stage matches in Mexico as opposed to in the United States.

stories.title: German public university creates Islamic theology faculty
stories.summary: The University of Münster is the first public university in Europe to establish an Islamic theology faculty, a move that is is attracting international attention.

stories.title: German coalition agrees on changes to pensions, tax rates
stories.summary: After its first year in office, Germany's coalition government plans to push through sweeping changes with the stated goal of reviving a sluggish economy. The plans met with a mixed reaction.

stories.title: AI drones made in Germany see duty on Ukraine's front line
stories.summary: The German defense company Helsing is supplying combat drones to Ukraine and will soon provide them to the German army. How effective are they on the front line? DW joined a Ukrainian combat mission to find out.

stories.title: As heat waves loom, scientists wonder how humans will adapt
stories.summary: Temperatures are set to soar in the US around the July 4 holiday, a week after Europe struck new records. With experts predicting more intense and more frequent heat waves in the future, can our bodies adapt?

stories.title: Sick leave: Germany rising but not the worst in Europe
stories.summary: Sick leave in Germany has hit a new record, with workers taking an average of nearly 20 days off per year. As Chancellor Merz cracks down, how does the country's absenteeism compare to other countries?

stories.title: Cheers and applause as security guard rescued from rubble
stories.summary: Emergency workers managed to free Hernán Gil more than 100 hours after they had first located him under 140 tonnes of rubble.

stories.title: Iran renews Hormuz threats as Doha talks end with cautious progress
stories.summary: Tehran threatened a "forceful response" against vessels straying from its approved Hormuz routes, even as mediators said indirect US-Iran talks in Doha concluded with "positive progress". The next round of talks will wait until after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral, which begins on Saturday.

stories.title: French aerobatic team stages flypast over New York for US anniversary
stories.summary: Patrouille de France flies over New York with Alpha Jets and coloured smoke during celebrations marking the 250th anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence.

stories.title: UN to close its human rights office in Burkina Faso following junta's suspension
stories.summary: Junta chief captain Ibrahim Traoré has pursued anti-Western policies since seizing power in a coup in September 2022, while critical voices are met with repression.

stories.title: Venezuelan security guard pulled alive from basement eight days after twin quakes
stories.summary: The earthquakes destroyed tens of thousands of buildings across northern Venezuela, killing more than 2,200 people, and leaving La Guaira state as the hardest-hit region in the country.

stories.title: Daredevil couple charged with felonies for Empire State Building proposal stunt
stories.summary: The highest public floor is the 102nd, where there’s an observation deck. Going higher requires a key card, according to the court complaint.

stories.title: Former Olympian indicted on felony charge over alleged Reflecting Pool vandalism
stories.summary: District of Columbia US Attorney Jeanine Pirro said David Hearn ripped up recently installed sealant on pool in “a deliberate act” that caused more than €875 in damage.

stories.title: A mayor in Japan announced her maternity leave - and got the whole country talking
stories.summary: In the face of criticism, Shoko Kawata, 35, says she loves her job and is proud to be taking time off to have a baby.

stories.title: After 250 years the American Dream is surviving, but only just
stories.summary: The American Dream has persisted since the days of the Founding Fathers, but faith in the ideal is fading

stories.title: On the Strait of Hormuz, BBC finds seized ships and shark fishermen as uneasy calm returns
stories.summary: The BBC travels to the city of Bandar Abbas - the first time journalists from a UK broadcaster have been to the Iranian side of the strait.

stories.title: 6.2 earthquake hits eastern Indonesia
stories.summary: The tremor hit at a depth of 120 kilometres, roughly 58 kilometres west of Tobelo in North Maluku province, and officials confirmed there is no tsunami threat.

stories.title: Israelis mark 1,000 days since 7 October attack
stories.summary: Thousands gathered in Tel Aviv to mark 1,000 days since the 7 October 2023 attack, as former hostages and bereaved families addressed the crowd.

stories.title: Firefighters battle major wildfire in southern France
stories.summary: A major wildfire in France's Aude department burns 900 hectares as aircraft and firefighters battle the blaze and evacuees reach emergency shelters.

stories.title: Portugal fans celebrate World Cup win over Croatia
stories.summary: Thousands of Portugal fans gathered in central Lisbon to celebrate a 2-1 victory over Croatia, sending their team through to the World Cup last 16.

stories.title: Rescued bile bears released in Denmark forest
stories.summary: Six rescued Asiatic black bears from South Korea start a new life in Denmark’s Knuthenborg Safaripark after an international rescue operation.

stories.title: Dogs don Argentina shirts as World Cup fever grips Buenos Aires
stories.summary: A dog walker in Buenos Aires leads dogs dressed in Argentina jerseys through the city as World Cup fever grows and fans celebrate in the streets.

stories.title: France records 2,025 excess deaths at peak of heatwave as Europe braces for more extreme weather
stories.summary: Forecasters are warning of further extreme temperatures on the continent in the next few days.

stories.title: Nasa launches mission to save falling space telescope
stories.summary: A Nasa-funded robot has blasted off to catch a falling telescope in mid-orbit and blast it back to safety before it burns up.

stories.title: Stand-up comic held for jokes about Erdoğan and Islam in Turkey crackdown
stories.summary: Deniz Göktaş is regarded as one of Turkey's most popular comedians and is accused of "inciting hatred and hostility" on stage.

stories.title: Iran to host dozens of foreign leaders for Khamenei's funeral, with Western nations absent
stories.summary: Russia's Medvedev, Pakistan's prime minister and Taliban officials are among roughly 30 country delegations expected as Iran buries slain Ayatollah Ali Khamenei this week.

stories.title: Côte d’Ivoire floods kill 59 as west Africa endures torrential rains
stories.summary: Authorities say rainy season getting deadlier, with Ghana reporting 13 dead and floods hitting Benin, Togo and Nigeria Floods in Côte d’Ivoire have killed 59 people since May, the communication minister told a cabinet meeting in Abidjan. There are fears the toll could further rise as rescue teams continue to search for victims during the rainy season, which runs from May until July, the minister, Amadou Coulibaly, added. Continue reading...

stories.title: Papua separatists kill American pilot in ‘message’ to US and Indonesia
stories.summary: Rebels shoot pilot and set his civilian ⁠plane on fire amid long-running low-level battle for independence in region Separatist rebels in Indonesia’s restive easternmost region of Papua have shot dead an American pilot and set a civilian ⁠plane on fire, in what a spokesperson for a local militant group described as a “message” to the US and Indonesian governments. Sebby Sambom, ⁠a spokesperson for the West Papua National Liberation Army (TPNPB), named the pilot as Nicholas F Gosselin and said se

stories.title: Explosion in Monaco: Who is Vadym Yermolaiev?
stories.summary: Ukrainian-born businessman Vadym Yermolaiev was reportedly injured in a suspected assassination attempt in Monaco. Kyiv had previously imposed sanctions on him. What is known about the case?

stories.title: EU looks to South Caucasus for solutions as von der Leyen holds talks with President Aliyev
stories.summary: Von der Leyen begins her South Caucasus tour in Baku as the EU steps up engagement on regional stability, energy and connectivity.

stories.title: Alleged member of hacking group 'Scattered Spider' arrested in Finland
stories.summary: Scattered Spider, also known as “Octo Tempest,” “UNC3944,” or “0ktapus,” is a group of criminal cyber actors that attempts to extort ransoms from companies.

stories.title: Smoke rises over Kyiv after Russian overnight strikes
stories.summary: Smoke rises over Kyiv after a large-scale Russian missile and drone attack overnight that killed at least eight people and injured dozens.

stories.title: EU urged to exempt UK from car rules that could be worst Brexit impact yet
stories.summary: Bloc’s ‘made in Europe’ regulations risk shutting out British manufacturers in ‘most spectacular own goal in history’ The EU’s car industry has called for the UK to be fully included in new “made in Europe” rules that threaten to shut out British manufacturers from their biggest export market. The European Automobile Manufacturers Association (Acea) on Wednesday urged Brussels to give the UK, Turkey and Morocco “justified, targeted exemptions” to the rules, which will require cars and parts to b

stories.title: Understanding Ebola’s wildlife origins is crucial to preventing next big outbreak
stories.summary: If we don’t know the source, not only do humans remain at risk but wildlife can suffer needlessly via retaliation While virologists and public health departments were palpitating over the news of an Andes virus infectious disease outbreak on a cruise ship (13 cases, three deaths), in the Democratic Republic of the Congo the Bundibugyo virus, the root of the current Ebola outbreak (currently more than 1,250 cases and at least 362 deaths), was smouldering under the radar. Bundibugyo virus is a hor

stories.title: Young Indonesian couple publicly caned after kissing on TikTok
stories.summary: Unmarried man and woman whipped 21 times each because they had violated province’s version of Islamic law A young couple in Indonesia’s conservative Aceh province have been publicly caned after a Sharia court convicted them of violating Islamic law by kissing during a TikTok livestream. The court ordered the couple, a 22-year-old man and a 25-year-old woman, to be whipped with a rattan cane 21 times each for kissing without being married. At least 100 people witnessed the caning, carried out by

stories.title: Chancellor's message to Germany's World Cup exit backfires
stories.summary: "You've inspired us," read a post by Chancellor Merz on X right after Germany's lackluster performance and early elimination from the FIFA World Cup. It is seen as a communication blunder that has repercussions.

stories.title: US declines to renew USMCA trade pact with Mexico, Canada
stories.summary: Lengthy negotiations are expected as the Trump administration seeks changes to a trade deal with Mexico and Canada that has streamlined supply chains across North America.

stories.title: Hospital fire in northern Germany kills two
stories.summary: A fire at a hospital in the northern German town of Ludwigslust has killed two people. Many patients and staff were evacuated from the facility.

stories.title: Why US trains trips are slower and worse than they should be
stories.summary: As flights get pricier and gas prices stay high, more US travelers are turning to trains — and hitting a system built for freight, not people.

stories.title: Fact check: Racist German fan petition story false
stories.summary: It has been claimed that following Germany's World Cup exit, some fans launched a petition seeking to ban "African" and "Muslim" players from the national team — and that DW reported the story. This is not true.

stories.title: Vatican excommunicates rebel SSPX bishops, followers
stories.summary: The Vatican's watchdog authority has imposed severe disciplinary measures on an ultratraditionalist Catholic group. The move comes after the group, the Society of Saint Pius X, consecrated bishops without papal consent.

stories.title: Did this AI anti-drug video make drugs look appealing?
stories.summary: Hong Kong’s Correctional Services Department removed an AI-generated K-pop video, after facing backlash that it made substances look too appealing.

stories.title: US-Iran parallel talks in Qatar end with pledge to keep talking
stories.summary: Holding separate meetings in Qatar, the US and Iran made progress and agreed that the next meeting would be after Khamenei's funeral, host Qatar confirmed.

stories.title: Venezuela's death toll at least 2,295 as medical crisis widens
stories.summary: Doctors fear a severe medical crisis in Venezuela following the 24 June earthquakes that killed 2,295 and injured more than 11,000. Thousands of displaced survivors are living in crowded shelters without clean water.

stories.title: 'Beautiful and terrible' - the world's thoughts on the US at 250
stories.summary: From Brazil to India to Egypt, the BBC asked people across the world what they think of America today.

stories.title: Russia's petrol crisis worsens as citizens feel the bite of Moscow's war in Ukraine
stories.summary: A third of Russia's refining capacity has been knocked out by Ukrainian drone attacks, causing petrol shortages across more than half of Russia's regions.

stories.title: Couple climbs Empire State Building, gets engaged, then arrested
stories.summary: Two climbers scaled the Empire State Building's antenna, displayed a peace banner and appeared to get engaged before being arrested by New York police.

stories.title: US fans celebrate World Cup last-16 spot
stories.summary: US supporters celebrate in Carson, California, after the United States beat Bosnia-Herzegovina 2-0 to reach the World Cup round of 16.
